steps.title: Find the matching punctuation pattern
steps.content: The opening comma after “become” signals that “with the breakout success of her 2020 novel Mexican Gothic” is an interrupting phrase that must be enclosed with matching punctuation on both sides.

steps.title: Complete the enclosing pair
steps.content:
A comma after “Gothic” matches the comma after “become” and properly closes the interrupting phrase, allowing “best known as a novelist” to follow.

The other choices use wrong punctuation (em-dash, semicolon) or provide none, breaking the sentence structure.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
